label: runner: rows ONLY on connect/listTools throw; diagnostic:null only when both succeed without throwing; SKIP row means liveOwner!=null (daemon alive at PID level), not that connect succeeded

Guard catches all-SKIP false green: when no runner:mk-spec-memory SKIP row (both connect cleanly), guard asserts 410 must be PASS/PARTIAL; if 410 SKIPs (missing memory_search), assertion fails and test fails — guard is effective

Only bypass of guard is PID recycling F-005: dead daemon's lease not cleaned + ownerPid recycled to live unrelated process -> isPidAlive returns true -> liveOwner!=null -> SKIP row -> guard bypassed. No other mechanism.

listTools() failure is NOT independent false-green path: both connect-throw and listTools-throw enter same catch block and are classified by liveOwnerForService only; zombie daemon -> SKIP (correct, PID IS alive); dead daemon -> FAIL (correct)

410 guard is specifically about tool availability not harness infra: guards against 'daemon connects but silently exposes no memory_search tools' scenario; this is correct behavior for a clean-env validation

direction: listTools zombie/unresponsive produces false-green SKIP without PID recycling
reason: isPidAlive only checks if PID is alive (process exists), not if daemon is responsive; zombie daemon -> liveOwner!=null -> SKIP is correct (PID IS alive, harness cannot spawn child)

direction: empty toolNames Set silently bypasses guard
reason: empty toolNames -> memory_search absent -> 410 SKIPs -> guard fires -> assertion fails (not silent pass)

direction: memoryScenario undefined (missing 410 row) silently bypasses guard
reason: undefined verdict -> expect(['PASS','PARTIAL']).toContain(undefined) throws -> test fails, not silent pass
